message:
fix(misc): benchmark temp-dir RAII, metadata-sorted backup, saturating clock, clippy median

Grab-bag of small, independent correctness fixes that came out of the
same audit pass. Committed together because they don't thematically
overlap with anything larger.

rch-wkr/src/main.rs (run_benchmark)
  Replaces the trailing `let _ = std::fs::remove_dir_all(&temp_dir)`
  with an RAII `DirGuard` struct that runs `remove_dir_all` on drop.
  Early-returns via `?` (write failures, spawn failures, toolchain
  errors) previously skipped the cleanup and leaked the temp build
  tree at `/tmp/rch-benchmark-<timestamp>/`. Benchmarks run on every
  worker selection round, so the leak compounds over a long-running
  daemon and silently fills /tmp.

rch/src/update/install.rs (find_latest_backup)
  Previously sorted directory entries by filename to find "the latest
  backup". That sort is lexicographic, which places `v1.0.10-...`
  *older* than `v1.0.9-...` (`0` < `9`) — so restoring "the latest
  backup" restored the wrong version once the point release rolled
  past 9. Worse, any segment with 10+ digits fell into an entirely
  different bucket.

  Fix: delegate to `list_backups()`, which reads each backup's
  `backup.json` metadata and sorts by the recorded creation time.
  "Latest" now means "most recently created", regardless of version
  string shape. Removes the custom dir-read + filename-sort logic
  entirely; the caller just takes `.next()` off a pre-sorted iterator.

rchd/src/api.rs (handle_status)
  `now - uptime_secs` used to compute the daemon's start time as an
  ISO-8601 timestamp for the status endpoint. If wall-clock stepped
  backward after startup (NTP correction), `uptime_secs > now` and
  the subtraction panicked in debug / wrapped in release, turning a
  cosmetic display value into either a crash or a year-1970 start
  time. `saturating_sub` pins the display at epoch when the clock
  step exceeds uptime — an obviously-wrong timestamp is preferable to
  a panic or an apparent time-travel.

rch-telemetry/src/benchmarks/network.rs (calculate_latency_stats)
  `if n % 2 == 0` → `if n.is_multiple_of(2)`. Silences the Rust
  1.87 `clippy::manual_is_multiple_of` lint and prevents future
  `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` rebuilds from failing CI. Identical
  semantics.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
